query: Render the architecture-heavy plan at the given plan directory with --static as a single shareable plan.html so a reviewer can see the phases, the system architecture, the key decisions and risks, and the files each phase touches without reading prose.
expected_behavior: Writes exactly one plan.html at the plan-directory root, beside plan.md, not under a visuals/ subdirectory
Renders a phase-timeline block where each phase card shows its title, status, priority, dependencies, and effort drawn from the phase frontmatter, so phase order and blocking are glanceable

Renders an architecture-diagram block as a client-side Mermaid diagram from each phase Architecture section, and embeds the Mermaid source in a visible <pre> fallback so the diagram content survives when the CDN is unreachable
Renders a decision/risk-cards block that surfaces Risk Map, Red Team Review, Key Insights, and Security Considerations as scannable cards rather than buried prose
Uses only the shared --wf-* theme tokens (no raw hex in block content) so the page reads consistently in light and dark mode
HTML-entity-encodes all plan-sourced text placed in the DOM; a plan containing <script> or on*= attributes renders it as literal text, not executable markup
A reviewer can identify the phases, the architecture, and the top decisions from the rendered page in under 30 seconds without reading paragraphs
